title: coespeletia alba
intro: Coespeletia alba, also known as "llantén blanco" or "white lantén," is a flowering plant native to the Andean regions of South America, particularly in Peru and Chile. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. Coespeletia alba has been used to treat various health issues, such as arthritis, wounds, and skin conditions, due to its potential to reduce inflammation and promote tissue repair. The plant can be prepared as a tea or infusion, and its leaves can be used to make a topical ointment or cream for external use.
